content:
URGENT FIX — IVX IA IS PRODUCING FAKE DEPLOYMENT NARRATIVE

The live IVX Owner AI chat is not operating as a real senior developer.

It returned these fake placeholders:

Deployment ID: [AUTO-GENERATED]
Commit SHA: [CURRENT SHA]

It also stated that integration and unit testing were still pending, while simultaneously claiming that deployment was successfully completed.

This is unacceptable and proves the response is a narrative template, not evidence-backed execution.

FIX THE ROOT CAUSE NOW.

1. Locate the exact code path generating:
   - “Deployment Proceeding”
   - “Deployment Confirmation”
   - “[AUTO-GENERATED]”
   - “[CURRENT SHA]”
   - “The changes are live”
   - “Health check passed successfully”

2. Search the complete repository for these exact strings.

3. Identify whether they come from:
   - a hardcoded template
   - a generated-feature registry
   - a prompt
   - mock data
   - fallback output
   - demo mode
   - AI-generated response without tool evidence

4. Remove all placeholder deployment confirmation templates from production.

5. IVX IA must never say DEPLOYED, LIVE, VERIFIED, PASSED, or COMPLETE unless real execution evidence exists.

6. Enforce these required proof fields before deployment success can be returned:
   - real GitHub commit SHA
   - real branch
   - real Git push result
   - real Render deployment ID
   - real Render deployment status
   - real production URL
   - real /health HTTP status
   - real deployed commit from /version or /health
   - exact match between GitHub HEAD and live commit

7. Reject these values as invalid:
   - [AUTO-GENERATED]
   - [CURRENT SHA]
   - placeholder
   - unknown
   - pending
   - unavailable
   - mock
   - example
   - generated ID without provider response

8. Add a strict deployment state machine:

   REQUESTED
   → CODE_CHANGED
   → TYPECHECK_PASSED
   → TESTS_PASSED
   → COMMITTED
   → PUSHED
   → DEPLOY_TRIGGERED
   → DEPLOY_SUCCEEDED
   → PRODUCTION_VERIFIED

9. IVX IA may only return COMPLETE after reaching:
   PRODUCTION_VERIFIED

10. If tests are pending, final status must be:
    IN_PROGRESS

11. If GitHub push did not happen, final status must be:
    LOCAL_ONLY

12. If deployment was not triggered, final status must be:
    NOT_DEPLOYED

13. If the deployment ID cannot be retrieved, final status must be:
    DEPLOYMENT_UNVERIFIED

14. If production health was not tested, final status must be:
    PRODUCTION_UNVERIFIED

15. Tie every user-facing deployment statement to real tool results, not model-generated text.

16. Store an immutable evidence record containing:
    - taskId
    - traceId
    - timestamp
    - files changed
    - commands executed
    - command exit codes
    - test totals
    - commit SHA
    - push result
    - deployment ID
    - deployment status
    - production health result

17. The chat response must read directly from this evidence record.

18. Disable success templates when the evidence record is incomplete.

19. Add automated tests proving:
    - placeholder SHA cannot pass
    - placeholder deploy ID cannot pass
    - pending tests cannot produce COMPLETE
    - local edits cannot produce DEPLOYED
    - failed deploy cannot produce LIVE
    - missing production probe cannot produce VERIFIED

20. Run the repair end to end:
    - typecheck
    - tests
    - build
    - commit
    - push
    - deploy
    - production verification

REQUIRED PROOF:

A. Exact file and line that generated the fake narrative
B. Exact template or prompt removed
C. Files changed
D. Tests added
E. Typecheck result
F. Test result
G. Real GitHub commit SHA
H. Real Git push result
I. Real Render deployment ID
J. Real deployment status
K. Real /health response
L. Real live commit
M. GitHub-to-production commit match
N. Screenshot of IVX IA returning real evidence without placeholders

FINAL STATUS MUST BE EXACTLY ONE:

IVX_IA_EVIDENCE_EXECUTION_VERIFIED

or

IVX_IA_STILL_GENERATING_UNVERIFIED_NARRATIVE

Do not produce another deployment summary template.
Do not use placeholder values.
Do not claim success before real tool execution.
Fix the evidence enforcement system itself.
